title: 命案目睹记
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\136-命案目睹记-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Fearing that a ticket inspector might dismiss her claims as the delusions of an elderly woman, Elizabeth McGillicuddy takes precautions before her train departs Blachampton. She writes a note on the back of a receipt, places it in an envelope, and pays a baggage handler a shilling to deliver it to the stationmaster's office.

An hour and five minutes later, after arriving at Milchester station, Mrs. McGillicuddy takes a taxi to St. Mary Mead. There, she meets Jane Marple and immediately informs her that she witnessed a murder. Miss Marple maintains her composure and invites Mrs. McGillicuddy to bathe and dine before discussing the matter in detail. During dinner, they discuss village rumors and Miss Marple's garden. Later, while sitting by the fireplace with dandelion wine and chamomile tea, Mrs. McGillicuddy recounts the terrifying event.

She describes seeing a woman, approximately thirty to thirty-five years old with blonde hair and a grayish-white fur coat, being strangled. The victim's face was distorted and her tongue was hanging out. The assailant was a tall man with dark hair wearing a thick coat; Mrs. McGillicuddy notes she did not see the man's face because he was facing away from her. Miss Marple theorizes that the killer likely got off at a nearby station, such as Blaketon, potentially hiding the body in a corner of the carriage under a fur collar. Miss Marple predicts the murder will appear in the next morning's newspaper, but the following morning, no such news is reported.
